Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Omega]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== The Captain ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 10''' — Omega is a being capable of dissolving his body into a gray mass and moving through Subspace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 10''' — Omega's human form appears to be a forty-year-old man with dark hair and a short beard.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 10''' — Omega has an Aethertech prosthetic left eye that shines orange.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 10''' — Omega is the Grave Hound.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 12''' — Omega is a character who can emerge from Subspace in human form.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 12''' — Omega possesses a cybernetic eye that blazes orange.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 12''' — Omega has an Aethertech body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 12''' — Omega can regenerate from gray ooze.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 15''' — Omega uses Subspace to store excess matter for regeneration.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 15''' — Omega possesses an internal Subspace Drive.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 15''' — Omega has an Aethertech eye.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 15''' — Omega can dissolve his body to slither away.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 23''' — Omega is the name used by the narrator to address the assassin.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 23''' — Omega can pop in and out of Subspace at will.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 23''' — Omega's regeneration is fueled by a limited stockpile of available mass.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 23''' — Omega is a potential entity responsible for distracting Magic Tower security.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 24''' — Omega is an attacker who uses a gun and fires bullets that trail energy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 24''' — Omega's bullets are capable of piercing normal starship armor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 25''' — Omega has a patch of steel surrounding his Aethertech eye.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 26''' — Omega is an assassin who calls himself the Grave Hound.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 26''' — Omega is at least a hundred and fifty standard years old.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 26''' — Omega's left eye is an Aethertech prosthetic embedded into a metal frame.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 26''' — Omega is a character being kept locked up.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 27''' — Omega is a character who spread information about the Captain of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 29''' — Omega is a prisoner who escaped from the ship's prison using a colorless copy of himself.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 29''' — Omega has orange eyes, one brighter than the other.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 29''' — Omega's regeneration is not infinite.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 35''' — Omega was relocated to a new holding facility.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 40''' — Omega is also referred to as Grave Hound or Omal.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 40''' — Omega is willing to attack a Hive for one hundred thousand standards.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 40''' — Omega is a famous assassin throughout the galaxy known for sabotage and assassination.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 41''' — Omega's goal is to go after Solstice.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 41''' — Omega is unable to pull a trigger when attempting to shoot the narrator.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 41''' — Omega's body turns into gray goo when shot by Sola.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 41''' — Omega can chuckle in three different voices at once.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 46''' — Omega can devour others and drag them through Subspace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 46''' — Omega can take a human form.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 47''' — Omega was hiding in the shadows during the encounter.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-01-the-captain|'''Ch 48''' — Omega used an enchantment-breaking gun that flashed orange.}}

== The Engineer ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 2''' — Omega is a crew member who is described as chaotic and rational.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 5''' — Omega is the Pilot of the crew and wants to defeat Solstice.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 7''' — Omega is a character capable of pushing Aethertech beyond its limits.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 9''' — Omega is a crew member on The Last Horizon who has Aethertech modifications fused with his flesh.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 9''' — Omega was previously kept in a jar in the vault.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 9''' — Omega is a character on the ship who can be kicked out.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 9''' — Omega is a character that Raion can relieve of his post.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 10''' — Omega is receiving medical treatment.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 18''' — Omega's request is to disable Solstice.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 18''' — Omega questions if the new Engineer is allowed six requests.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 19''' — Omega is a member of the crew.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 22''' — Omega is the hired murderer of the Escalon Company.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 22''' — Omega is immortal.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 22''' — Omega can spread dark gray biomass to form faces.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 22''' — Omega views Raion as a fun challenge to unravel his persona.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 24''' — Omega is the source for Subspace Drives in the envisioned fleet.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 27''' — Omega is a character who eats during the flight to his destination.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 27''' — Omega has gray ooze flowing from his back that forms mouths.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 27''' — Omega can assemble a gun from his dark ooze.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 27''' — Omega has both a human eye and an artificial eye.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 27''' — Omega is capable of vanishing by folding into himself.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 28''' — Omega is a dark ooze that can morph into a human-like form.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 28''' — Omega can communicate through a feed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 29''' — Omega is a pilot on the Zenith Starship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 30''' — Omega possesses scanners.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 32''' — Omega is a member of the crew who is bound by a contract.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 32''' — Omega is a gray ooze with an orange eye.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 32''' — Omega uses a treadmill.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 34''' — Omega is bound by contract magic.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 34''' — Omega has an Aethertech eye that blazes orange in excitement.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 36''' — Omega is a mercenary who previously attacked Varic Vallenar at the Magic Tower.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 41''' — Omega suggests killing Starhammer or someone else.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 43''' — The Advocates have contact with Omega.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 43''' — Omega can melt into ooze.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 45''' — Omega has a restriction against murder reinforced by contract magic.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 50''' — Omega hijacked a series of vessels and then abandoned them in space.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 50''' — Omega can pull a gun out of his Subspace storage.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 51''' — Omega knows that the narrator's students found him once.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 52''' — Omega is an ooze-man.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 52''' — Omega can use lasers to remove cuffs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-02-the-engineer|'''Ch 54''' — Omega can ooze into wounds.}}

== The Knight ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 3''' — Omega is a crew member who communicates via Subline connection.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 3''' — Omega uses a unique Subspace warp to travel.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 3''' — Omega is a crew member who uses Aethertech eyes and guns.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 3''' — Omega uses Subspace to store his guns.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 4''' — Omega is a character that can slither away.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 4''' — Omega vanished.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 8''' — Omega is a member of the crew and sits at an orange-lit wedge of the table.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 8''' — Omega can sprout eyes from fifteen tendrils of dark gray ooze.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 14''' — Omega can keep Horizon company.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 15''' — Omega is aboard The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 17''' — Omega is someone who needs to be woken up.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 18''' — Omega operates the Pilot console.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 18''' — Sola lost eyes on Omega.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 18''' — Omega was on the ship's console surrounded by bodies.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 22''' — Omega is a character who can partially liquefy himself and sprout eyes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 23''' — Omega is a character who was being transmitted audio to.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 23''' — Omega is a character on the ship who can dance onto the bridge.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 23''' — Omega's real name is Omal.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 28''' — Omega is someone Varic orders to be contacted.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 28''' — Omega is a character who can link himself to the Pilot console.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 35''' — Omega was dancing from foot to foot.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 36''' — Omega is a crew member who operates a console.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 36''' — Omega's body is partially composed of a dark gray ooze that can reform.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 36''' — Omega is a man with rare Aethertech and at least a century of experience.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 36''' — Omega has a Subspace inventory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 37''' — Omega is part of an away team sent aboard enemy ships.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 37''' — Omega was partially destroyed, with only the top-right quarter of his body remaining intact.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 38''' — Omega is a crew member who operates scanners and the Subspace Drive.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 39''' — Omega chuckles in a half-dozen different voices.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 39''' — Omega is capable of evading debris and enemy fire while moving through a changing gap in a worm's body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 40''' — The narrator saved Omega for last.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 42''' — Omega wears an orange-and-white uniform which is the combat suit of the Titan Knights.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 42''' — Omega refers to the narrator as Captain.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-03-the-knight|'''Ch 43''' — Omega is an entity that the narrator interacts with.}}

== The Pilot ==
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 3''' — Omega is a combatant who uses an optical implant to provide feeds.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 3''' — Omega was standing over a bloody body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 4''' — Omega has a mouth that forms on a tendril extending from his shoulder.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 4''' — Omega chews gum and spins a handgun.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 8''' — Omega is kept in a secure vault.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 8''' — Omega is a character capable of manifesting in the form of black ooze.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 8''' — Omega can manifest in a human form using dark goo to hold himself in place.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 14''' — Omega is Aila Escalon's father.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 14''' — Omega has a form of regeneration.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 15''' — Omega is not aboard the ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 15''' — Omega can sprout new mouths.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 16''' — Omega is a device or entity used for sensors.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 16''' — Omega is located inside Shadow Ark.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 18''' — Omega pooled on the ceiling of the Divine Titan’s cockpit.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 18''' — Omega is a being capable of oozing across ceilings and manifesting his upper body.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 18''' — Omega can manifest a tongue from his finger.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 24''' — Omega can keep most of his mass tucked away in Subspace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 24''' — Omega has another mouth on the side of his neck.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 25''' — Omega has taken control of the cargo ship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 25''' — Omega is a character who operates a starship.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 26''' — Omega is an admitted assassin for hire.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 28''' — Omega speaks in several voices at once.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 29''' — Omega has eyes on stalks.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 29''' — Omega can resist the call of Subspace longer than a normal human.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 29''' — Omega is a pilot who can manifest multiple pairs of hands.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega helped the Pilot escape an encirclement by the defenders of Fathom.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega is the Pilot.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega is the World Spirit.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega fought his daughter.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega can reconstitute himself after being shot.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega fought Aila and the Zenith Blade.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega is a Pilot who has been described as an insane killer.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega's knuckles bleed black goo.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega can pull a glowing orange gun from Subspace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 30''' — Omega can fold himself into darkness and disappear into Subspace.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 32''' — Omega is the Pilot of The Last Horizon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 32''' — Omega has completed discreet assignments for Benri Vallenar.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 32''' — Omega is a character who spoke about Solstice.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 34''' — Omega is known as the Grave Hound and was the founder of Escalon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 35''' — Omega is a valuable source of information against the enemy.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 35''' — The narrator is tasked with finding Omega.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 36''' — Omega can melt into goo and flow through wall channels to escape.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 36''' — Omega's supply of mass responds sluggishly when low.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 38''' — Omega is a character who infiltrated Benri's peers by disguising himself as an underling.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 40''' — Omega is from the Zykaros system.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 40''' — Omega is a crew member who can roll six extra eyes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 40''' — Omega lost his daughter, family, reputation, and fifty years of his life to Solstice.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 40''' — Omega wears a mask.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 40''' — Omega is a provisional crew member.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 41''' — Omega uses Aethertech in his ears.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 42''' — Omega is a passenger on the shuttle and acts as the helm.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 42''' — Omega is Jak's grandfather.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 42''' — Omega has a metallic eye that can expand.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 44''' — Omega is someone Aila Escalon has fought.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 46''' — Aila Escalon is the daughter of Omega.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 47''' — Omega has a clone of his daughter.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 47''' — Omega can enhance the thrusters of The Last Horizon as the Pilot.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 47''' — Omega can dissolve into black goo.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 48''' — Omega can wrap himself in a missile to blow up an Iron Hive and pull himself back together.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 48''' — Omega is a character who would find it hilarious if he died for the ruin of his own home planet.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 48''' — Omega previously stated that a stronger contract eats a weaker contract.}}
* {{spoiler|book=lh-04-the-pilot|'''Ch 49''' — Omega was gone.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
